comments: End of show missing. Tommy announces AC/DC at the start of the show, it is assumed the missing track at the end is one of theirs.
This week's FNC set by Farley Havicon, Redhill

sessions.artist: Def Leppard session for Andy Peebles
sessions.details: Recorded: 1979-06-07. First TX: 1979-06-18. First FRS TX 07 August 1981. No known commercial release.

sessions.artist: Budgie FRS Session 1st TX (recorded "a fortnight ago")

track_listing.track: 'We Will Rock You' (LP - Live Killers) EMI
'Hold On' (LP - Live In Texas: Dead Armadillos) Aura
'Belfast' (v/a LP - The Friday Rock Show) BBC Records
'Brute Force And Ignorance	' (LP - Stage Struck) Charisma
'Rocka Rolla' (LP - Hero Hero) Gull (Cuts during outro)

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Hello there, this is TV on the Radio here, Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
Okay, tonight two Welsh bands and one from the northern part of our country in session.
From way back in 1973 we have Man, from last year Def Leppard, and recorded a fortnight ago, we've got Budgie.
Top of that, I've got some new stuff by Trapeze, by Witchfind, Persian Risk, Harvest Moon, Kiss, and ACDC.
Some classic cuts by Rory Gallagher, Thin Lizzy, and Judas Priest, among others,
who will be featured in the Friday Night Connection.
Got the answer to last week's and a brand new one as well.
But this evening, we start with this.
That's a band by the name of Trapeze and a track called Hold On from an album that's recently come out on the Aura label.
The album is called Live in Texas, Dead Armadillos.
And if it's the same trapeze who started recording way back in 1970, they've certainly got their act together again.
And before that, you heard something by Queen from their album called Live Killers.
The track was We Will Rock It.
And that was especially for a lady by the name of Diane Cowley.
She's from Bolton.
She goes out with a fellow in Hoik, a fellow by the name of Beans.
And also for all the Genesis fans who've come down to London for this weekend's concerts.
The 19th, I think they're all going.
They come from Aberdeen and somewhere else.
Oh, Aberdeen in Scotland.
Well, it's always been there.

who lives normally at 41 Sycamore Place in Aberdeen.

and Andrew Walton and Russell L.
and Michelle and Simon Armand.

who lives in St. Osith in Essex.

who lives in Cafes Encarnate.

recorded a fortnight ago in the BBC studios.
The track was called You're A Superstar.

and his mate Richard C. Rankin,
who live in Plumley in Cheshire.

who wants me to say hello and a Merry Christmas

Tell them not to drink too much, says Kit.
Would they? Of course they would.
It's Kit's birthday tomorrow.
Happy birthday for tomorrow, Kit.
And I must say this.
Thanks to everybody who's sent in Christmas cards.
It's very kind of you, and I greatly appreciate it.
A little bit of advice with regard to next week's programme,
which, of course, is on Christmas Day.
Do not miss it.
The whole two hours is based around ACDC.
But now here's something out of the archives, recorded back in 1973.
This is Man and Ain't There Fight.
It was Man, and it caught me by surprise, for which I apologise.
It was a track that was recorded back in 1973, and it was called Ain't There Fight.
And I'm just looking through the record books to see the number of people who were in Man, and it's absolutely amazing.
Deke Leonard, of course, was in. Terry Williams was actually on that session.
He's currently, I think, playing drums for Meatloaf over in America.
And then Martin Belmont, who is in Graham Parker.
Dave Edmonds was in the band, BJ Cole.
It's incredible, like a sort of Welsh Mafia.

And as I said, that session track was recorded way back in 1973.
